description.task: Write a Java program that uses a for loop and a break statement to find and print the first multiple of 7 that is also greater than 50.
instructions: Inside the loop, check if i % 7 == 0.
If true, assign i to found and use break to exit the loop.
Print the result in the exact format shown.
description.hint: break immediately exits the innermost loop. After break, execution continues at the line after the loop.

description.task: Write a Java program that uses a for loop and a continue statement to print only even numbers from 1 to 20.
instructions: Inside the loop, use an if statement to check if i is odd (i % 2 != 0).
If the number is odd, use continue to skip to the next iteration.
If the number is even, print it.
Print all even numbers from 2 to 20, each on its own line.
description.hint: continue skips the rest of the current loop iteration and moves to the next step in the loop header (i++).

description.task: Write a Java program with a method that checks if a number is prime. Use return to exit early when a divisor is found.
description.context: return inside a method immediately stops execution and sends a value back to the caller — it can be used as an early-exit strategy in loops within methods.
instructions: Loop from i = 2 to i < n.
If n % i == 0, return false immediately (uses 'return' as an early exit).
Do NOT change the main() method.
description.hint: You can optimize by looping only up to Math.sqrt(n): for (int i = 2; i <= Math.sqrt(n); i++). This is more efficient but not required.
description.expectedOutput:
17 is prime: true
18 is prime: false
1 is prime: false
